confirmed_errors_ranked.error: BoolQ 'pure label-prior shift' headline is REVERSED by the full n=3264 data and by the analyzer's own gold-aligned metric. paper_draft_v5.md:188-189 and SHARPENING_FINDINGS.md:13 state extract on BoolQ is 'a near-perfect label-prior shift (gold=yes +0.328 / gold=no -0.326, net approx 0), not discrimination.' Re-running the project's own analyze_boolq.py on the saved results/exp10_boolq/*/results.json (n=3264 each), the gold-ALIGNED margin (logit(gold)-logit(other), which the verdict rule on analyze_boolq.py:116 is built on) gives, for extract: gemma3_12b gold=yes +0.916 / gold=no +0.782 -> REAL SHARPENING; qwen25_7b +0.390/+0.489 -> REAL SHARPENING; ministral_8b +0.450/+0.001 -> REAL SHARPENING; only qwen25_1_5b +0.403/-0.326 -> prior shift. So 3 of 4 models, including gemma (the model the downstream story depends on), show real discrimination, not a prior shift. I bootstrapped gemma3_12b: gold=yes +0.916 CI[+0.76,+1.07], gold=no +0.782 CI[+0.62,+0.94], both exclude 0. The +0.328/-0.326 numbers are a STALE qwen25_1_5b interim (the -0.326 matches qwen1.5b's full-run gold=no exactly; +0.328 matches no full-run model). The unconditional mean P(yes) shift on gemma is only +0.008 -- there is essentially no prior shift to speak of.
confirmed_errors_ranked.fix: Replace the +0.328/-0.326 'prior shift, net~0' headline in paper_draft_v5.md:188-189, paper_draft_v4.md:252, and SHARPENING_FINDINGS.md:13 with the per-model full-run gold-aligned margins. State that extract priming produces a small but REAL discrimination gain on BoolQ for the larger instruct models (gemma12b, qwen7b, ministral8b); the pure-prior-shift pattern holds ONLY for qwen25_1_5b. The Section 5 mechanism story ('priming only shifts the label prior on abstract classification, not discrimination') must be retracted or heavily qualified -- it is true for one weak model only.

confirmed_errors_ranked.error: BoolQ accuracy AND calibration deltas are NOT null, contradicting SHARPENING_FINDINGS.md:16-17 ('all accuracy/calibration deltas null'). On the full n=3264 run (independently recomputed and via analyze_boolq.py), extract improves accuracy and BALANCED accuracy (the prior-immune metric): gemma3_12b Delta_acc +0.009 [+0.003,+0.016]*, Delta_bal_acc +0.008 [+0.002,+0.014]*, Delta_ECE -0.007*, Delta_Brier -0.008* (all five CIs exclude 0, all improving); ministral_8b Delta_acc +0.017 [+0.01,+0.02]*, Delta_bal_acc +0.012*. Balanced accuracy rising is the signature that distinguishes real sharpening from a prior shift (a pure yes-shift would inflate yes-recall but kill no-recall, leaving balanced accuracy flat/down). Contextual-calibration-adjusted accuracy (divide out the content-free prior) still favors extract (gemma +0.009 cal, ministral +0.009 cal), so the prior-shift confound is partially controlled and a real effect survives. This is the same root cause as the prior-shift error: an un-updated qwen25_1_5b first-interim was generalized to all models.
confirmed_errors_ranked.fix: Report the per-model full-run Delta_acc / Delta_bal_acc / Delta_ECE / Delta_Brier with bootstrap CIs and remove the 'all deltas null' claim. Merge with the prior-shift fix above: the corrected BoolQ statement is 'extract priming yields a small significant discrimination + calibration gain on larger instruct models, not merely a prior shift.'

confirmed_errors_ranked.error: The abstract/Section 6.1 'clean double dissociation by content type' (Gemma imprints meaning but 'cannot store arbitrary literals'; Qwen imprints surface form/codes) holds only for the single cherry-picked pair gemma3_12b vs qwen25_7b (exp27, which has exactly two model dirs). I reproduced the §6.1 table exactly from exp27. But the CODE dimension was run on all 11 models in exp26, and the full sweep contradicts the surface half: recomputing content=code_strip-code_neutral from results/exp26_bank_semantic (N=150, bootstrap CIs): gemma3_27b code = -0.330* (Gemma DOES store a literal), mistral_7b -0.565* (a paper-labeled 'semantic imprinter' banks code MORE than qwen7b), qwen25_7b -0.373*, qwen25_14b +0.781* (strongly ANTI-banks), qwen25_1_5b +0.180* (anti-banks). The Qwen surface axis is non-monotonic -- only the cherry-picked 7B banks code. The SEMANTIC axis is the robust finding: monotonic Gemma scaling (-0.57/-2.46/-3.62/-3.77 for 1b/4b/12b/27b), Mistral on the line (-1.36), r(imprintability, semantic_banking)=0.945 reproduced. The model's own log (SHARPENING_FINDINGS.md:770-793) flags 'double dissociation too strong... CODE banking is idiosyncratic/non-monotonic (no law)' -- but this correction was applied to the semantic axis and NOT propagated to the abstract's surface claim.
confirmed_errors_ranked.fix: Rewrite the abstract and §6.1: present the SEMANTIC axis (imprintability r=0.94, monotonic Gemma scaling, Mistral on the line) as the clean, robust result, and present the surface/code axis as model-specific and idiosyncratic, NOT 'clean.' Drop 'Gemma cannot store arbitrary literals' (gemma27b code -0.33*) and the 'Qwen imprints surface form' generalization (qwen14b +0.78*, mistral banks code more than Qwen). Disclose the full-sweep code-banking column. Use the base-vs-instruct mode-flip (§6.4, exp26 -- independently reproduced: qwen sem -0.72->+0.14, code +0.17->-0.37*) as the principled vehicle for the surface-imprinting claim instead of the cherry-picked pair.

confirmed_errors_ranked.error: The pooled tfidf d(margin)~0.00 presented as evidence that 'the keyword win vanishes / is an entropy artifact' (paper_draft_v5.md:176,187) is a SIGN-CANCELLATION artifact across models, not a per-sample null. Recomputing per-model from results/exp05_discrimination, the tfidf margin effect is large and individually significant with OPPOSITE signs: qwen25_1_5b +0.243 (z=+12.1, helps strongly), mistral_7b +0.104 (z=+4.3), gemma3_12b -0.240 (z=-6.6, HURTS), ministral_8b -0.071 (z=-6.2). Pooling Cohen's d across models with opposite signs averages to ~0, which is then read as 'no discrimination effect.' This both hides a real bidirectional effect and directly conflicts with the paper's OWN later thesis that imprinting mode is model-specific (the same Qwen-helps / Gemma-hurts split appears here). The generic_instr win (pooled +0.294) is also heterogeneous (mistral +0.568 to ministral +0.074) but at least same-signed.
confirmed_errors_ranked.fix: Report the tfidf margin effect PER MODEL, not pooled. State that keyword priming's margin effect is real but model-specific and bidirectional (helps low-imprintability Qwen, hurts high-imprintability Gemma), which is consistent with -- and foreshadows -- the imprinting-mode thesis, rather than 'the keyword win is a pooled null/entropy artifact.' Pool only same-signed conditions, or report a model x condition interaction.

confirmed_errors_ranked.error: The contrastive margin is claimed to be 'entropy-invariant' (paper_draft_v5.md:149-150, used 5x; build_discrimination_sweep.py:16-19). This is mathematically FALSE for the actual confound it controls. Entropy reduction in an LM is multiplicative logit-space sharpening (temperature L->L/T), not a uniform additive NLL offset. I reproduced the simulation: under temperature scaling, margin = (logit_correct - mean(logit_distractor))/T, so margin scales as 1/T -- a pure T=1.0->0.5 sharpening DOUBLES the margin (verified ratio exactly 2.00; T=0.3 ratio 3.33), with no rank change and no new information. The margin is invariant ONLY to a constant additive NLL offset, which is not how 'the prefix makes the model globally more confident' manifests.
confirmed_errors_ranked.fix: Reword all 5 occurrences: say the margin is 'invariant to a uniform additive NLL shift' and explicitly note it is NOT invariant to temperature/sharpening. Back the metric instead with the empirical control that actually works on the saved exp05 data: the lockstep test (tfidf: correct and distractor NLL fall together, d_rank~0 -> sharpening; generic_instr: correct NLL down but distractor NLL UP, anti-lockstep -> genuine) plus the temperature-invariant rank/top1 measures. This changes no number and no empirical conclusion.

methodology_theory_issues: SINGLE-PAIR GENERALIZATION is the recurring structural flaw. Three headline dissociations all rest on the SAME cherry-picked pair gemma3_12b vs qwen25_7b: (a) §6.1 content-type spectrum (exp27 has exactly 2 model dirs), (b) the downstream QA mode-task reversal (exp29 has 3 dirs; the load-bearing contrast is 12b +0.358* vs 7b -0.800*), and (c) implicitly the reranking-vs-QA 2x2. Where the relevant dimension WAS run on the full sweep (code banking, exp26), it contradicts the 2-model story. Any claim labeled 'clean double dissociation' needs the full ladder before the word 'clean' is used.

STALE-INTERIM CONTAMINATION: the BoolQ +0.328/-0.326 numbers (a qwen1.5b first-interim) propagated unchanged into v4, v5, and SHARPENING_FINDINGS while the full 4-model x 3264 run sat completed on disk contradicting them. Process gap: findings docs and paper were not re-derived from final results. Recommend an automated 'regenerate all paper tables from results/ JSON' script so no hand-copied interim survives.

POOLED EFFECT SIZES MASK SIGN-HETEROGENEITY: pooling Cohen's d or per-sample means across models whose true effects have opposite signs (tfidf margin) produces a spurious ~0 that is read as a null. Given the paper's own thesis is that the effect is model-specific, pooling across models is the wrong default; report per-model with an explicit interaction test.

THE 'ENTROPY-INVARIANT' METRIC JUSTIFICATION IS UNSOUND AS STATED (additive vs multiplicative). The metric empirically works here (lockstep + rank/top1 backstop it), but the stated reason is wrong, and the paper never cites the lockstep/rank evidence that is the actual control. Kauf et al. 2403.14859 (external) further warn the contrastive margin only removes confounds if distractors are TRUE minimal pairs (matched length/frequency/tokenization); the paper should audit distractor construction for surface matching, or the margin re-imports the entropy/length confound it claims to remove.

MACHINERY CONTROL IS LOAD-BEARING BUT THIN: bankability/content-type/downstream all subtract a 'neutral=newlines' prime to remove the reposition+normalize machinery cost. The machinery cost is large and model-dependent (gemma3_4b QA machinery +0.92*, gemma12b +0.16*, qwen7b +0.00), so the content effect is a difference of two noisy large quantities. On the smallest/most-perturbable models (gemma4b) the machinery swamps the content signal (content +0.116 n.s. under machinery +0.92). The per-tensor-normalize step may be partly compensating for a positional/scale shift introduced by RoPE reposition rather than a true content effect (CacheBlend/Block-Attention/KVLink show naive reposition causes residual loss); an ablation isolating reposition vs normalize vs priming is needed.

BASE-MODEL IMPRINTABILITY METRIC IS MIS-CALIBRATED ACROSS BASE/INSTRUCT: the imprintability predictor (|Delta query-NLL| from a generic 'extract' prefix) under-reads base models because they don't follow the instruction (gemma4b-base reads 0.21 yet banks semantic -1.11*). The r=0.94 correlation and the §6.4 base-vs-instruct comparison therefore use a metric that behaves differently across the very base/instruct boundary the mode-flip claim depends on (echoing Kauf et al.'s base-vs-instruct LogProb shift). Same-footing calibration of the imprintability metric across base/instruct is required before attributing the flip purely to training.

IMPRINTABILITY r=0.94 IS FRAGILE (n=8) AND POSSIBLY GENERIC STEERABILITY: with 8 models the CI is wide and the correlation is dominated by the Gemma ladder (4 of 8 points, monotonic by construction). It may be a restatement of general steerability/ICL-capacity (Belief Dynamics reports r~0.97 cross-model in this space). Need leave-one-out / CI on r, and a control showing imprintability predicts SEMANTIC banking better than it predicts a content-neutral steerability baseline.

NOVELTY OVERLAP with task-aware-cache literature is under-cited: 'Beyond RAG' (2503.04973), CacheNotes, and Cartridges (2506.06266) already build task-aware, query-agnostic caches offline; Memory Inception explicitly names 'discard the scaffolding prefix' as a baseline it rejects (and finds architecture-dependence, echoing our mode result). The paper should be framed as a MECHANISM study (what priming banks, model-specifically) not as a new task-aware cache, and must add the SnapKV-selection-with-task-prefix baseline -- if selection alone matches priming, the mechanism is redundant.
refinement_ideas: Regenerate every paper table and SHARPENING_FINDINGS number from results/*/results.json with a single deterministic script (bootstrap, fixed seed). This alone catches the stale-interim BoolQ error and any other hand-copied numbers; make it a CI check so no interim can ship.

Convert the BoolQ result from a liability into a positive: report per-model full-run gold-aligned margins, balanced accuracy, and contextual-calibration-adjusted accuracy with CIs. The corrected story ('extract yields a small REAL discrimination + calibration gain on larger instruct models; pure prior shift only on qwen1.5b') is a stronger, more honest claim than the retracted 'pure prior shift.'

Replace pooled cross-model effect sizes with a model x condition mixed-effects model (random slope per model). Report the tfidf margin as a model-specific bidirectional effect; this turns the sign-cancellation embarrassment into direct corroboration of the imprinting-mode thesis (Qwen-helps / Gemma-hurts, the same axis as semantic banking).

Strengthen the central semantic axis: it is the robust survivor (monotonic Gemma scaling, Mistral on the line, r=0.945, base-vs-instruct flip all reproduced). Promote it to THE headline, demote surface/code to 'model-specific, idiosyncratic.' Add leave-one-out CI on r=0.94 and a steerability-control regression so the correlation is not dismissable as generic ICL capacity.

Add the missing reposition/normalize ablation: measure content-bankability with (i) reposition only, (ii) normalize only, (iii) both, vs a no-reposition retain-window baseline, to prove per-tensor-normalize is banking content rather than compensating a positional/scale artifact (the CacheBlend/KVLink risk). Reuse exp25's machinery-control harness.

Audit distractor construction (exp05) for length/frequency/tokenization matching per Kauf et al. 2403.14859; report margin_first (length-invariant) alongside margin_mean for every cell (the saved data already has *__margin_first) to show no length confound flips a sign.

Same-footing the imprintability metric across base/instruct (use a content-prime banking probe, not an instruction-following 'extract' prefix, so base models are not under-read) before resting the §6.4 mode-flip on it.

Run the SnapKV-with-task-prefix selection baseline as a required control; if task-guided EVICTION matches priming's gains, say so. This is the single most important missing baseline per the literature review and pre-empts the 'redundant mechanism' reviewer objection.

task_aware_plan.strategy: Mode-matched prefix routing: pick prefix STYLE per (model, task) using the imprinting-mode trait. Semantic/instructional prefix for high-imprintability semantic imprinters (Gemma >=12B, Mistral) on RELEVANCE tasks; surface/keyword (TF-IDF or question-token) prefix for surface imprinters (Qwen-2.5-instruct) on EXTRACTION tasks.
task_aware_plan.rationale: Grounded in the two robust, independently-reproduced results: (a) per-passage keyword priming beats bare on gemma12b/27b reranking (+0.033*/+0.036* MRR, verified on exp14c N=900) while generic priming HURTS (-0.026 to -0.037*); (b) the downstream QA mode-task reversal (exp29, verified): priming a passage with the question helps qwen7b extraction (-0.800*) but hurts gemma12b (+0.358*). Mode is model-specific and the trait predicts it (r=0.94). Literature: this is the 'choose the offline task description by model' refinement of Beyond RAG / DynamicKV, which assume one prefix policy fits all models.
task_aware_plan.experiment: Build a (model, task)->prefix lookup. Cells: {gemma3_12b/27b, mistral_7b, qwen25_7b} x {MS MARCO/HotpotQA reranking, SQuAD/DROP extractive QA}. Prefix variants: generic-extract, per-passage-tfidf-keywords, question-tokens, semantic-summary. Reuse exp14c (reranking MRR) and exp29 (QA content-effect, machinery-controlled with neutral=newlines) harnesses unchanged. Score: ΔMRR vs bare (reranking), Δanswer-NLL content-effect vs neutral-strip (QA). Predict each cell's winning prefix from the model's imprintability/mode BEFORE running; report routing accuracy.
task_aware_plan.prediction: FALSIFIABLE: keyword/surface prefix wins for Qwen-QA and semantic/keyword-passage prefix wins for Gemma-reranking, while the cross (semantic prefix on Qwen-QA, surface prefix on Gemma-reranking) is null-or-negative. Routing by the imprintability trait beats a single-global-prefix policy by >=+0.02 MRR averaged over relevance cells. CONCRETE NULL THAT KILLS IT: if the best prefix is the same across models within a task (i.e., it's a task property not a model-mode property), mode-routing adds nothing -- exactly the disentanglement the audit flags as needed.

task_aware_plan.strategy: Offline margin-driven prefix SEARCH: because the task is fixed and the query is not, search a small pool of candidate prefixes (templates, summaries, few-shot exemplars, TF-IDF keyword sets) offline and select the one maximizing mean contrastive margin over a held-out pool of synthetic task queries; build the cache once.
task_aware_plan.rationale: The task being known offline makes search free at inference (mirrors Beyond RAG's task-description choice, but OPTIMIZED not hand-picked). Our data shows prefix CONTENT matters enormously and non-monotonically (generic hurts reranking, keyword helps; entity-priming backfires on capable models per SHARPENING_FINDINGS deep-dive-3) -- so hand-picking is fragile and a margin-guided search should dominate. Uses the contrastive margin (corrected to report rank/top1 alongside, per error #5).
task_aware_plan.experiment: On a task with a synthetic query pool (SQuAD passages with held-out Q/A), enumerate ~20 candidate prefixes per passage, score each by mean(margin) over 10 synthetic queries on a TRAIN split, pick argmax, evaluate on a disjoint TEST query split. Baselines: bare, generic-extract, oracle (best prefix chosen on test). Reuse exp05/exp11 scoring. Report TEST margin AND rank/top1 (temperature-invariant) to prove the search found discrimination, not just sharpening.
task_aware_plan.prediction: FALSIFIABLE: searched prefix beats generic-extract on TEST margin AND on rank/top1 for the semantic imprinters; for surface imprinters the search converges on keyword/question-token prefixes. If the search only improves margin but NOT rank/top1 (pure sharpening) it has overfit to the entropy confound -- a clean falsification that the gain is illusory. Prediction: capable Gemma models will favor a generic/summary prefix over specific entity-targeting (SHARPENING_FINDINGS shows specificity backfires), so the search recovers the 'simple broad cue wins' result automatically.

task_aware_plan.strategy: Task-prefix SELECTION + PRIMING combined: use the task description to BOTH SnapKV/Ada-KV-select which doc KV to retain AND prime/reshape the retained KV, then discard the prefix.
task_aware_plan.rationale: The literature's most important missing baseline: SnapKV/KVzip show most task benefit comes from WHICH KV you keep (selection), and the auditors flag that if selection alone matches priming, our mechanism is redundant. This experiment directly measures priming's marginal value OVER selection, and -- if positive -- yields a method stronger than either alone and a clean differentiation from Beyond RAG (which only selects).
task_aware_plan.experiment: Three arms at matched cache budget on MS MARCO reranking + SQuAD QA: (A) SnapKV-select-with-task-prefix only; (B) our prime+reposition+normalize only (full cache); (C) select-then-prime. Models: gemma3_12b (primable), qwen25_7b (control). Reuse the v4 pipeline lib for reposition/normalize and add a SnapKV scorer. Metric: ΔMRR / Δcontent-NLL vs bare at equal budget.
task_aware_plan.prediction: FALSIFIABLE: on gemma3_12b, C > A and C > B (priming adds value beyond selection AND selection adds value beyond priming) -> the mechanisms are complementary, justifying the paper. If A >= C (selection captures all the benefit), priming is REDUNDANT and the mechanism claim collapses -- the decisive test. On qwen7b (non-semantic-imprinter) predict priming adds nothing to selection for reranking.

task_aware_plan.strategy: Cross-attention-preserving reposition: keep a tiny number k=1-4 of prefix KV slots (attention-sink style) instead of discarding the prefix entirely, so retained doc tokens keep the positional/attention anchor they were computed against.
task_aware_plan.rationale: CacheBlend/PromptCache/KVLink show repositioning precomputed KV after dropping cross-chunk attention causes measurable loss; our machinery cost (~0.3-0.6 nats, verified ~0.16-0.92 across models in exp29) may be exactly this artifact. If per-tensor-normalize is silently compensating a positional shift (not banking content), a few retained sink slots should recover most of the machinery loss at near-zero cost -- and would turn the QA-extraction REGRESSION on Gemma (+0.358*) toward neutral.
task_aware_plan.experiment: Bankability + downstream-QA harness (exp25/exp29), vary retained prefix slots k in {0 (current), 1, 2, 4}, with and without per-tensor-normalize (2x4 grid). Models gemma3_4b (highest machinery cost +0.92), gemma3_12b, qwen25_7b. Metric: machinery cost (neutral-strip minus bare) and content effect, as a function of k.
task_aware_plan.prediction: FALSIFIABLE: machinery cost shrinks monotonically with k and the k-slot+no-normalize condition matches 0-slot+normalize -> proves normalize was compensating a positional artifact (a mechanistic clarification the paper currently lacks). If normalize still helps at k=4 beyond what slots recover, normalize is doing real content work. Prediction: k=2-4 sink slots recover >=half the Gemma machinery cost and flip gemma3_12b QA content-effect from +0.36 toward 0.

task_aware_plan.strategy: Distill priming into a short LEARNED soft prefix optimized once per task to maximize held-out contrastive margin, then discarded after priming (training-free-priming -> Cartridges bridge).
task_aware_plan.rationale: Cartridges (2506.06266) sets the gradient-based quality bar; reviewers will ask why not train a cartridge if our training-free gains are a few hundredths MRR. A short soft prefix optimized ONLY to maximize the contrastive margin (not full context distillation) is a cheap middle ground that, per our finding that prefix content is the active lever, should substantially beat hand-written prefixes for a fixed task -- giving the paper a stronger, still-cheap upper operating point.
task_aware_plan.experiment: Per task, learn a length-16 soft prefix by gradient descent to minimize mean answer-NLL (or maximize margin) over a synthetic train-query pool, freezing the model; prime each document with it, reposition+normalize, discard, evaluate on held-out queries. Compare vs best hand-written prefix and vs bare. Models gemma3_12b, qwen25_7b. Reuse exp11/exp29 scoring; add a tiny prefix-tuning loop.
task_aware_plan.prediction: FALSIFIABLE: learned soft prefix > best discrete prefix on held-out margin AND rank/top1 for the semantic imprinter, at <1% of Cartridges' compute. If the learned prefix only improves margin (sharpening) but not rank/top1, or fails to beat the discrete prefix, the training-free ceiling is genuinely near the bankability limit (~65% un-bankable) and soft-prefix optimization cannot exceed it -- which is itself a publishable bound.

headline_claims_status.claim: Absolute NLL is entropy-confounded for evaluating priming; use a contrastive margin = mean(NLL_distractor)-NLL_correct.
The keyword/TF-IDF 'win' is a pooled entropy artifact (margin d~0.00) -- only generic instructions improve discrimination.
On BoolQ, priming produces only a near-perfect label-prior shift (gold=yes +0.328/gold=no -0.326, net~0), not discrimination; all accuracy/calibration deltas null.
Clean double dissociation by content type: Gemma imprints meaning but 'cannot store arbitrary literals'; Qwen imprints surface form (codes) but not meaning.
The SEMANTIC axis: priming banks semantic content into doc KV, scaling monotonically with Gemma size (-0.57/-2.46/-3.62/-3.77), present in Mistral, weak/absent in Qwen.
Imprintability (mean |Delta query-NLL| from a generic prefix) predicts semantic banking at r=0.94 across 8 models.
Retained context is ~-2.8 nats and mostly un-bankable (>=65% lost); content-bankable fraction is small/model-dependent (~0 Gemma, ~14% Qwen).
Reranking: per-passage keyword priming beats bare on Gemma 12B/27B (+0.036 MRR), null elsewhere; generic priming hurts.
Downstream mode-task match: semantic imprinting (Gemma) helps reranking but HURTS QA extraction (+0.36 nats); surface imprinting (Qwen) helps QA (-0.80 nats).
Imprinting mode is set by instruction-tuning, not architecture: base models universally semantic-imprint; Qwen 2.5's tuning flips it to surface (sem -0.72->+0.14, code +0.17->-0.37).
Five architectural accounts of imprintability falsified (QK-norm, sharpness, prefix-salience, fixed-direction, norm-control).
